skill.name: first-responder-program-finder
skill.description: Use when an agent needs to navigate the FirstResponderHomePrograms website UI to find statewide verified programs, under-review signals, free deeper-opportunity teasers, or paid Research Vault and workspace information.
skill.summary: Navigate a first responder home programs website to find verified mortgage assistance and down payment programs by state and role.
skill.author: daanaagua

security_audit.summary: All 142 static analysis findings are false positives. The static scanner incorrectly interpreted markdown backtick formatting (e.g., `State`, `Role`) as Ruby/shell command execution across 112 locations. The 26 'system reconnaissance' flags were triggered by benign navigation terms like 'finder' and 'search' in documentation. The single 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flag at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive on YAML front matter text. The only confirmed risk factor is network access: the skill references URLs to firstresponderhomeprograms.com, which is the intended target website for navigation. The skill consists entirely of markdown documentation with no executable code.

low_findings.title: External Website URLs in Skill Definition
low_findings.description: The skill references the external website firstresponderhomeprograms.com for navigation purposes. URLs are hardcoded in SKILL.md and README.md. This is expected and safe behavior for a website navigation skill, but constitutes a network risk factor.

content.user_title: Find first responder home programs by state
content.value_statement: Finding verified first responder home buyer programs requires navigating complex state-level information. This skill guides agents through the official website to locate verified programs, under-review signals, and deeper opportunities for any state and role.
actual_capabilities: Navigate the homepage finder to search by state and role
Identify verified statewide first responder home programs
Check for under-review official program signals
Detect deeper local or specialty opportunities behind the paid layer
Guide paid users through the Decision Pack workspace and Research Vault
Distinguish between free public information and paid research records
limitations: Cannot provide mortgage quotes or eligibility guarantees
Does not access internal APIs or modify website code
Cannot perform lender underwriting or pre-approval decisions
Paid workspace details require direct source review and user judgment for historical or conditional records

use_cases.title: Find verified programs for a first responder
use_cases.description: A first responder wants to know which home buyer programs are available in their state for their specific role. The agent uses the homepage finder to search by state and role, then reviews verified matches and under-review signals.
use_cases.target_user: First responders seeking home buyer assistance

use_cases.title: Research statewide program options
use_cases.description: A housing counselor needs an overview of all first responder home programs in a specific state. The agent navigates to the state page, reviews verified statewide programs, under-review signals, and deeper opportunity counts.

use_cases.title: Access detailed research workspace
use_cases.description: A paid user needs local, specialty, or employer-assisted program details beyond the free layer. The agent guides the user through the Decision Pack workspace, Research Vault, and comparison tools.
use_cases.target_user: Paid subscribers needing detailed research access

prompt_templates.title: Basic program search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find first responder home programs in Texas for firefighters.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants to quickly check available programs in their state.

prompt_templates.title: State overview with under-review check
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me all first responder home programs in Connecticut, including any programs under review and deeper opportunities.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user wants a comprehensive view of programs in their state, including those not yet verified.

prompt_templates.title: Zero match troubleshooting
prompt_templates.prompt: I searched for EMT programs in Ohio and got zero verified matches. What should I check next?
prompt_templates.scenario: A user receives no results and needs guidance on broadening their search and checking alternative signals.

prompt_templates.title: Paid workspace navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an active paid subscription. Guide me through the Research Vault to find local first responder programs in my county, including saved scenarios and comparisons.
prompt_templates.scenario: A paid user wants to access detailed local research, saved items, and comparison tools in the workspace.

output_examples.input: Find first responder home programs in Texas for firefighters.
output: Start on the homepage finder at the root path.
Enter Texas as the state and firefighter as the role.
Review verified matches that appear in the results.
If zero verified matches appear, reset Support type and Repayment to Any.
Expand under-review signals to check for pending programs.
Check for deeper opportunities in Texas.

output_examples.input: What programs are under review in Connecticut for first responders?
output: Navigate to the Connecticut state page.
Review the under-review official signals section.
Check the verified statewide program list for any matching entries.
Note that under-review programs are cautionary and may change.
Check deeper opportunities for additional local or specialty options.

best_practices: Always start with the homepage finder and enter State and Role before adjusting optional filters.
Reset Support type and Repayment to Any when zero verified matches appear before concluding no programs exist.
Clearly distinguish between verified programs, under-review signals, and deeper opportunities when presenting results to users.
anti_patterns: Do not present the website as an instant quote tool or lender recommender.
Do not claim that the site has confirmed user qualification or lender approval.
Do not treat every Research Vault record as a verified, broadly available current program.

faq.question: What information is available for free?
faq.answer: The free layer includes the homepage finder, state pages with verified statewide programs, under-review signals, and a teaser count of deeper opportunities.

faq.question: What does the paid workspace add?
faq.answer: The paid workspace provides access to the Research Vault, saved scenarios, saved programs, comparisons, conflict checks, question kits, and action checklists for local, specialty, and employer-assisted programs.

faq.question: What should I do when the finder shows zero verified matches?
faq.answer: Reset the Support type and Repayment filters to Any, then check under-review signals and the deeper opportunities teaser before concluding no options exist.

faq.question: Is this site a lender or mortgage provider?
faq.answer: No. The site is an information resource that helps first responders find verified home programs. It is not a lender, underwriter, or pre-approval tool.

faq.question: What state input formats are accepted?
faq.answer: The state field accepts full state names like Connecticut, two-letter abbreviations like CT, and is case-insensitive.

faq.question: Are all Research Vault records verified programs?
faq.answer: No. The Research Vault may include current opportunities, items needing judgment, and historical or blocked records. Not every record is a verified, broadly available current program.
